Spending money on exclusive lanes and priority signaling also makes them faster and more dependable. Nonetheless, constructing and retaining such infrastructure requires capital and long-term vision. Equity issues are also important: lines need to serve low-income communities along with commercial hubs so that all citizens have a benefit. Finally, when properly planned, public transportation promotes economic efficiency, social mobility, and a cleaner city. SSC Stenographer 06/08/2025 (Shift 2)
passages.p47: Universal immunization campaigns have eradicated or controlled fatal conditions such as smallpox and polio. Mass immunization relies on cold-chain availability: vaccines have to be stored at precise temperatures from manufacturing through search on TG -@freepdfhall inoculation. Solar-powered refrigerators and insulated containers are lifelines for remote locations. Door-to-door interactions by community health workers educate households regarding dosage routines and myths. Vaccine hesitancy fueled by misinformation is also a major obstacle to herd immunity. Campaigns of public education— capitalizing on radio, social media, and school initiatives—seek to revive public faith in science. Preserving accessibility and communication in an open nature is crucial in order to secure public health. SSC Stenographer 06/08/2025 (Shift 3)
passages.p48: Microfinance institutions (MFIs) have become crucial agents for advancing financial inclusion in rural areas. Through extending small loans and savings accounts to people who don't have access to conventional banks, MFIs empower entrepreneurs—most often women—to initiate or scale up microenterprises. In most villages, these loans support poultry farming, handloom weaving, or tiny retailing outlets. MFIs go beyond credit by regularly offering financial education training, teaching borrowers to cope with cash flows and interest charges. Critics warn, however, that excessive interest rates and repayment pressure can result in over-indebtedness. To avoid such risks, some MFIs limit interest rates and spread repayment terms. Research indicates that when complemented by market linkages—such as guaranteed buyers for fruits and vegetables —microfinance can trigger sustainable economic growth and poverty reduction. SSC Stenographer 06/08/2025 (Shift 3)
passages.p49: As artificial intelligence (AI) technologies continue to proliferate across every sector of human activity, the ethical implications of their deployment have become a matter of urgent concern. Once relegated to the realm of speculative fiction, AI now permeates decision-making in fields as diverse as healthcare, finance, education, and criminal justice. While proponents argue that AI can increase efficiency, reduce human bias, and democratize access to services, critics warn of the potential for algorithmic discrimination, erosion of privacy, and the deepening of existing social inequalities. At the heart of the debate lies a fundamental tension between technological progress and moral responsibility. Machines, no matter how sophisticated, lack consciousness and intrinsic values; they merely execute instructions based on data inputs and programmed algorithms. Thus, when an AI system makes a life-altering decision —such as denying a loan application or predicting a defendant's likelihood of reoffending—it is not the machine itself that is accountable, but the human architects behind its creation and training. Moreover, the opacity of many AI systems, often referred to as the "black box" problem, poses a significant ethical challenge. When even developers cannot fully explain how a machine arrived at a given outcome, the prospect of ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability becomes deeply problematic. Addressing these concerns will require not only technical innovations, such as explainable AI, but also rigorous interdisciplinary collaboration between engineers, ethicists, policymakers, and the public. SSC Stenographer 07/08/2025 (Shift 1)
passages.p50: Changes that seem small and unimportant at first will compound into remarkable results if you're willing to stick with them for years. We often convince ourselves that massive success requires massive action. Whether it's losing weight, building a business, or learning a new skill, we put pressure on ourselves to make some earth-shattering improvement that everyone will notice. However, improvement by just 1 percent each day may not be noticeable in the moment, but over time it can be far more meaningful. The same way that money compounds with interest, the effects of your habits multiply as you repeat them. The outcomes you see in your life today are often the result of small choices made over time, rather than dramatic changes. This is why habits are the compound interest of self-improvement. The longer you stick with them, the greater the returns. But just as positive habits can compound in your favor, negative habits can compound against you. A slight slip in discipline today may not seem like a big deal, but if repeated, it can steer you in a completely different direction. Success, then, is not a single event-it is the result of daily choices, made consistently over time. SSC Stenographer 07/08/2025 (Shift 2)
passages.p51: Freedom of speech is a cornerstone of democratic societies, allowing individuals to express ideas, question authority, and promote change. However, in the digital world, this right encounters complex challenges. Social media platforms have become arenas where opinions can be voiced instantly-but also where hate speech, misinformation, and cyberbullying thrive. Balancing the right to speak freely with the responsibility to prevent harm is now a pressing debate. Should platforms censor harmful content, or would that violate freedom? While some argue that regulation is essential to protect vulnerable groups, others fear it might lead to suppression of dissent. The problem deepens when private companies-not elected bodies - decide what speech is allowed or banned. Striking the right balance requires thoughtful policies, digital literacy, and legal frameworks rooted in both human rights and public safety. In a world where words travel faster than thought, protecting free speech without enabling harm may be one of the defining tests of modern democracy. SSC Stenographer 07/08/2025 (Shift 2)
passages.p52: In the age of endless options-from streaming services to social media platforms-modern individuals face what psychologists call "decision fatigue." Unlike earlier generations who had limited choices, today's youth are constantly required to decide: what to watch, whom to follow, what to share, and how to respond. While freedom of choice is often celebrated, too many choices can lead to stress, dissatisfaction, and even paralysis. Ironically, abundance can become overwhelming. Algorithms, designed to predict preferences, often narrow exposure rather than broaden it, creating digital bubbles. People may feel in control, yet their choices are subtly shaped by unseen patterns. Over time, the constant need to choose can drain mental energy, leaving individuals anxious or indifferent. True digital freedom may lie not in limitless options, but in mindful engagement and setting personal boundaries. Recognising when to choose-and when to step back-could be the new wisdom of the connected age SSC Stenographer 07/08/2025 (Shift 3)
passages.p53: Much of the discussion in this book is about biases of intuition. However, the focus on biases does not mean that intuition is always wrong-far from it. Most of the time, the quick judgments of our intuitive mind, what I call System 1, are accurate enough to help us navigate the world. This system operates automatically and quickly, with little or no effort. On the other hand, System 2 is the slower, more deliberate mode of thinking. It allocates attention to effortful mental activities, such as solving complex problems or evaluating logical arguments. System 2 is more methodical and rational, but it is also lazier-it prefers to endorse the intuitions of System 1 rather than question them. This division of labour works well most of the time, but it also creates predictable errors. Because System 1 is prone to biases and snap judgments, we often make decisions that are not as rational as we believe. Understanding these two systems can help us recognize when we are relying too much on intuition and when we need to engage in more deliberate thinking. SSC Stenographer 07/08/2025 (Shift 3)
passages.p54: In a small village, a government school has become an example of clean energy use. The school uses solar panels to run fans, lights, and even computers. This helps the school save money on electricity bills. The saved money is used to buy books and improve classrooms. The teachers also explain to students how solar energy works and why it is good for the planet. Students learn that solar power does not cause pollution like coal or petrol. The school has also inspired nearby houses to install solar panels. This change has made the village cleaner and more aware of the environment. Even on cloudy days, the solar battery stores enough power to keep the school running. With small steps like this, more schools and homes can help protect nature and build a better future. SSC Stenographer 08/08/2025 (Shift 1)
passages.p55: The rise of genetic engineering has redefined what is scientifically possible, but not necessarily what is ethically acceptable. While gene editing offers hope in treating inherited diseases and improving crop resilience, it also raises profound moral questions. Should we edit genes to enhance intelligence or physical ability? Where do we draw the line between therapy and enhancement? These questions remain unsettled. Some argue that altering the human genome for non-medical reasons may lead to a new form of inequality—where the genetically modified have social advantages over others. Others fear unintended consequences: once a gene is changed, its effects may ripple through generations. Science often moves faster than legislation, leaving ethical frameworks lagging behind. True progress lies not in what we can do, but in what we choose to do responsibly. As with all powerful tools, genetic engineering demands caution, reflection, and a shared sense of human values. SSC Stenographer 08/08/2025 (Shift 1)
passages.p56: Theme: The Dandi March Sector: Indian History / Freedom Movement In 1930, Mahatma Gandhi started a peaceful protest called the Dandi March. He and his followers walked 240 kilometers from Sabarmati Ashram to Dandi, a village near the sea. The purpose of the march was to protest the British law that taxed salt. Salt is a basic need for everyone, and Gandhi believed it was unfair to make people pay for it. During the march, more and more people joined him. The British government tried to stop the movement, but Gandhi remained calm and peaceful. He made salt from the sea at Dandi, breaking the law in a non-violent way. The Dandi March became a strong symbol of India's fight for freedom. It showed the world that peaceful protest could be powerful. Even today, it is remembered as a brave step in India's struggle for independence. SSC Stenographer 08/08/2025 (Shift 2)
passages.p57: As algorithms increasingly curate our digital experiences — what we read, watch, buy, or even whom we date — they quietly shape our perception of the world. This phenomenon, known as "algorithmic living," promises efficiency but risks eroding autonomy. When choices are filtered through predictive systems, personal agency may weaken, and individuals might mistake convenience for control. Moreover, algorithmic personalization often traps users in feedback loops, reinforcing existing preferences and limiting exposure to diverse perspectives. Over time, this can lead to cognitive narrowing, where users become less open to alternative views or new ideas. While algorithms are not inherently harmful, their unchecked influence raises ethical concerns about manipulation and passive consumption. True digital well-being demands critical awareness — not of the machine, but of how we engage with it. In a world increasingly optimized for us, the question is not what the algorithm shows, but whether we are still choosing to look beyond it. SSC Stenographer 08/08/2025 (Shift 2)
passages.p58: In today's digital age, convenience often comes at the cost of privacy. Every time a user browses a website, clicks a link, or uses an app, their data is collected —sometimes without their knowledge. Companies use this information to target ads, predict behavior, or even sell insights to third parties. While this can lead to better services, it also raises ethical concerns. Who controls this data? How is it stored, and can users truly opt out? Many people accept terms and conditions without reading them, unknowingly giving permission for data tracking. Some countries have passed laws to protect user data, but implementation remains weak. As surveillance tools grow smarter, the line between public and private becomes blurred. Individuals must now learn to balance the benefits of technology with the right to privacy—a task that requires awareness, regulation, and responsible digital behavior. SSC Stenographer 08/08/2025 (Shift 3)
passages.p59: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is gradually transforming the way students learn. From virtual tutors to personalized learning paths, AI helps identify each learner's strengths and weaknesses. With the help of data, AI systems can suggest specific topics where students need improvement. Schools are also using AI chatbots to answer basic queries, reducing the pressure on teachers. However, experts warn that over-dependence on AI may limit critical thinking. If students rely too much on machines for answers, they may lose problem-solving skills. There are also concerns about data privacy, especially when student performance data is stored online. Despite these issues, many educators believe AI can be a useful tool when used wisely. Like any technology, the key lies in balancing human guidance with machine efficiency. SSC Stenographer 08/08/2025 (Shift 3)

questions.question: What is the main focus of epigenetics according to the passage?
options: The study of gene expression without changing DNA.
questions.solution: The study of gene expression without changing DNA. दिया गया passage कहता है , "epigenetics reveals that environmental factors... can profoundly influence gene expression without altering the underlying DNA code" यह स्पष्ट रूप से main focus को identify करता है।

questions.question: What do DNA methylation and histone acetylation do in epigenetics?
options: They turn genes on or off.
questions.solution: They turn genes on or off. दिया गया passage कहता है कि ये modification, molecular स्विच के रूप में कार्य करते हैं , genes को on या off करते हैं , या उनके activity level को शंशोधित (modulate) करते हैं।

questions.question: How does epigenetics affect our idea of fixed genetic destiny?
options: It shows DNA sequences always change.
It shows the environment can change inherited traits.
It proves all twins are exactly alike.
It only focuses on physical looks.
questions.solution: It shows the environment can change inherited traits. दिए गये passage में उल्लेख किया गया है कि ‘epigenetic tag’ पीढ़ियों (generation) तक विरासत (inherit) में मिल सकते हैं , और यह fixed genetic destiny की दीर्घकालिक धारणा (long-held notions) को challenge करता है , जिसका अर्थ है कि environment इस बात को प्रभावित कर सकता है कि हमें क्या विरासत में मिलता है।

questions.question: What does "malleable score" mean for our genetic inheritance?
options: A set plan that cannot be changed.
A flexible genetic plan that life experiences can change.
questions.solution: A flexible genetic plan that life experiences can change. दिया गया passage कहता है कि विरासत (inheritance) एक कठोर खाका (blueprint) नहीं है , बल्कि एक लचीला स्कोर (malleable score) है जिसे जीवन (life) द्वारा लगातार पुनर्संयोजित (re-orchestrated) किया जाता है , जिसका अर्थ है कि यह flexible है और जीवन द्वारा परिवर्तित होता है।

questions.question: Which of these is NOT something we learn from epigenetics, according to the passage?
options: That genes are always stronger than environment.
questions.solution: That genes are always stronger than environment. दिया गया passage प्रकृति (nature) एवं पोषण (nurture) के बीच गतिशील अंतर्संबंध (dynamic interplay) की बात करता है जहाँ पोषण प्रकृति को प्रभावित कर सकता है , जो इस विचार के विपरीत है कि genes हमेशा प्रभावी (dominant) होते हैं। अन्य options को benefit के रूप में mention किया गया हैं।

questions.question: What is the main idea of the passage?

questions.question: What are the economic implications of declining pollinator populations mentioned in the passage ?

questions.solution: Lower crop yields and higher food costs (Passage से ली गयी Line/s - Many fruits, vegetables, and nuts rely heavily on animal pollination, and a reduction in pollinator services can lead to lower yields, reduced quality, and increased food costs./ कई फल , सब्जियां और मेवे भारी मात्रा में पशु परागण पर निर्भर करते हैं , और परागणकर्ता सेवाओं में कमी से कम उपज , कम गुणवत्ता और बढ़ी हुई खाद्य लागत हो सकती है। )

questions.question: What is the author's primary purpose in writing this passage ?

questions.question: What is the primary argument made by critics against a liberal arts education?
options: It is too expensive compared to STEM degrees.
It fails to provide immediate job readiness and specialized skills for the modern workforce.
It does not encourage intellectual curiosity.
It focuses too much on science and technology.
questions.solution: It fails to provide immediate job readiness and specialized skills for the modern workforce. दिया गया passage बताता है कि आलोचकों (critics) का तर्क है कि liberal art की degree अव्यावहारिक (impractical) हैं क्योंकि वे graduates को समकालीन नौकरी बाजार (contemporary job market) के लिए आवश्यक विशिष्ट कौशल (specific skill) के साथ तैयार नहीं करती हैं।

questions.question: According to proponents, what is a key benefit of a liberal arts curriculum regarding critical thinking?
options: It narrowly focuses on specific industry skills.

Resisting all forms of regulatory changes.

Business को लचीलापन (resilience) बनाने की दिशा में आगे बढ़ना अनिवार्य है , जिसमें मजबूत जोखिम प्रबंधन ढांचे (robust risk management frameworks) में invest करना , आपूर्ति ठिकानों (supply bases) में विविधता लाना , तथा व्यापक अस्थिरता को प्रभावी रूप से नेविगेट करने के लिए निरंतर सीखने (continuous learning) एवं नवाचार (innovation) को बढ़ावा देना शामिल है।

questions.question: What is the central idea of the passage?
options: Algorithms have made decision - making more objective.
Digital systems subtly influence human perception and autonomy.
The internet is an entirely neutral platform for expression.
questions.solution: Digital systems subtly influence human perception and autonomy. दिए गए passage का central idea यह बताता है कि algorithm और digital system मानव विकल्पों , व्यवहार एवं धारणा (perception) को कैसे आकार देते हैं जबकि स्वायत्तता का भ्रम (illusion of autonomy) पैदा करते हैं। अतः , option (c) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: The phrase “illusion of choice” suggests that—
options: online options appear diverse but are algorithmically curated.
questions.solution: online options appear diverse but are algorithmically curated. दिए गए passage में phrase "illusion of choice" यह संदर्भित करता है कि internet उपयोगकर्ताओं को कई विकल्प होने का आभास search on TG -@freepdfhall (appearance) कैसे देता है , जबकि वास्तव में , algorithm, control और filter करते हैं कि वे क्या देखते हैं। अतः , option (b) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: What does the author imply about artificial intelligence ethics?
options: It fully resolves issues of bias.
questions.solution: It mirrors existing human flaws. लेखक का कहना है कि artificial intelligence अपने creators और उसके द्वारा consume किए जाने वाले data के पूर्वाग्रहों (biases) को विरासत में प्राप्त करता है , यह दर्शाता है कि AI मानव पूर्वाग्रह (human prejudice) तथा अपूर्णता (imperfection) को दर्शाता एवं पुनरुत्पादित (reproduces) करता है। नैतिक समस्याओं (ethical problems) को हल करने के बजाय , यह अक्सर मानव निर्णय में पाए जाने वाले समान दोषों (same flaws) को दर्शाता है। अतः , option (c) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the paradox mentioned in the passage ?
options: Data has lost all ethical significance.
questions.solution: Technology isolates and empowers simultaneously. दिया गया passage सशक्तिकरण (empowerment) के विरोधाभास (paradox) को describe करता है , अर्थात् मानवता आज knowledge और connectivity तक अभूतपूर्व पहुंच (unprecedented access) रखती है , फिर भी पहले से कहीं अधिक हेरफेर (manipulation) के प्रति संवेदनशील (vulnerable) है। यह technology के dual nature को दर्शाता है , यह लोगों को information के साथ सशक्त (empower) बनाता है जबकि साथ ही उनकी स्वायत्तता (autonomy) को कम करता है और उन्हें नियंत्रण के अधीन करता है। अतः , option (a) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: The author’s tone in the passage can best be described as—
questions.solution: Alarmist yet hopeful दिए गए passage में , लेखक algorithm के बढ़ते प्रभाव , मानव धारणा (human perception) के साथ छेड़छाड़ , और artificial intelligence की नैतिक चुनौतियों (ethical challenges) के बारे में चेतावनी देते हुए एक alarmist लेकिन hopeful tone में बात करता है , जबकि अभी भी यह आशा व्यक्त करता है कि मानवीय मूल्यों (human values) की पूर्ति के लिए technology का पुनः मानवीयकरण (rehumanized) किया जा सकता है। अतः , “Alarmist yet hopeful” सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: Beyond income gaps, what are other dimensions of inequality mentioned in the passage ?
options: Access to luxury goods and international travel.
Access to quality education, healthcare, and digital literacy.
questions.solution: Access to quality education, healthcare, and digital literacy. दिए गए passage में कहा गया है कि असमानता (inequality) केवल income के अंतर से आगे बढ़कर quality education, healthcare और digital literacy तक असमान पहुंच को भी शामिल करती है।

questions.question: What specific challenges do women in India frequently face, despite legislative protections?
options: Systemic discrimination, violence, and a significant wage gap.
A disproportionately high participation in the formal workforce.
questions.solution: Systemic discrimination, violence, and a significant wage gap. दिए गये passage में कहा गया है , " महिलाएं ... अक्सर प्रणालीगत भेदभाव (systemic discrimination), लिंग - आधारित हिंसा (gender-based violence) और एक महत्वपूर्ण वेतन अंतर (wage gap) का सामना करती हैं। "

questions.question: What dual burden does India's public health infrastructure battle?
questions.solution: Communicable and non - communicable diseases. (Passage से ली गयी Line/s - India battles a dual burden of communicable and non-communicable diseases./ भारत संक्रामक और गैर - संक्रामक रोगों के दोहरे बोझ से जूझ रहा है। )

questions.question: How does environmental degradation, such as pollution and deforestation, primarily impact society in India ?
options: It benefits urban areas by attracting investment.
It disproportionately affects the poorest segments and agricultural communities.
It has no significant impact due to economic growth.
It leads to an increase in natural resources.
questions.solution: It disproportionately affects the poorest segments and agricultural communities. दिया गया passage बताता है कि पर्यावरणीय विघटन (environmental degradation) कृषि समुदायों (agricultural communities) तथा उन लोगों को असम्मानजनक रूप से प्रभावित करती है जो प्राकृतिक संसाधनों (natural resources) और समाज के सबसे गरीब तबके (poorest segment) पर निर्भर हैं।

questions.question: What is ultimately suggested as a measure of progress for Indian society?
options: The rate of industrialization and urbanization. afforded to every individual.
The number of new technological advancements.
questions.solution: The well-being, equity, and dignity. दिया गया passage यह निष्कर्ष निकालता है कि एक ऐसे समाज को बढ़ावा देने की प्रतिबद्धता की मांग की जाती है जहां प्रगति को केवल आर्थिक संकेतकों (economic indicators) से नहीं , बल्कि प्रत्येक व्यक्ति को प्रदान की गई भलाई (well-being), equity और गरिमा (dignity) से मापा जाता है।

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the tone of the passage ?
questions.solution: Analytical एक analytical tone में , लेखक किसी topic के बारे में सभी fact और reason को देखता है और उन्हें clear और organized way में समझाता है। दिया गया passage तर्कपूर्ण तुलना और संतुलित तर्क के माध्यम से मौलिकता (originality) और अनुकरण (imitation) दोनों की जांच करता है। अतः , “Analytical” सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: The author suggests that imitation can serve as—
options: A foundational process for achieving mastery
A meaningless repetition of prior work
questions.solution: A foundational process for achieving mastery ( Passage से ली गयी Line/s - To imitate with discernment is to study the anatomy of excellence; it is the apprenticeship through which mastery often gestates./ समझदारी के साथ अनुकरण करना उत्कृष्टता की संरचना का अध्ययन करना है ; यह वह प्रशिक्षुता है जिसके माध्यम से अक्सर महारत विकसित होती है। )

questions.question: What does the author imply by “fetishization of originality”?
options: Society’s excessive glorification of originality at the expense of learning from the past
The rejection of innovation in favor of traditional methods
The belief that imitation alone leads to mastery
The effort to balance creativity with discipline
questions.solution: Society’s excessive glorification of originality at the expense of learning from the past Phrase "fetishization of originality" का तात्पर्य है कि समाज ने मौलिकता (originality) के प्रति एक जुनून (obsession) या अत्यधिक महिमामंडन (over-glorification) विकसित किया है , इसे कुछ पवित्र या श्रेष्ठ के रूप में मानते हुए। लेखक इस रवैये (attitude) की आलोचना (criticizes) करता है क्योंकि यह रचनाकारों (creators) को अनुकरण (imitation) और अतीत के ज्ञान से सीखने के महत्व को अनदेखा करने का कारण बनता है। अतः , option (a) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: What is meant by the phrase “an echo refracted through time” ?
options: Every creative idea carries traces of the ideas that came before it.
Innovation always replaces the traditions of the past.
Modern art rejects all historical influences.
Time weakens the value of older creative works.
questions.solution: Every creative idea carries traces of the ideas that came before it. इस phrase का अर्थ है कि रचनात्मकता (creativity) कभी पूरी तरह से नई नहीं होती है ; यह शुरुआती विचारों और कार्यों से प्रभावित और आकारित होती है , ठीक एक प्रतिध्वनि (echo) की तरह जो समय के साथ यात्रा करते हुए थोड़ा बदल जाती है। अतः , option (a) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: From the author’s perspective, true creativity arises when—
options: Originality is pursued in isolation from imitation
The old and the new are synthesized into meaningful innovation
The creator rejects all prior influence
questions.solution: The old and the new are synthesized into meaningful innovation दिए गए passage से यह निष्कर्ष निकलता है कि creativity एक बुद्धिमान स्मरण (intelligent remembrance) का कार्य है , जो स्थायी नवाचार (innovation) उत्पन्न करने के लिए मौलिकता को अनुकरण (imitation) के साथ मिश्रित करती है। अतः , option (c) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: What was the primary focus of early AI research ?
questions.solution: Symbolic reasoning and expert systems ( Passage से ली गई Line/s - early AI research, emerging in the mid-20th century, focused on symbolic reasoning and expert systems./ प्रारंभिक AI अनुसंधान , 20 वीं शताब्दी के मध्य में उभर रहा था , प्रतीकात्मक तर्क और विशेषज्ञ प्रणालियों पर केंद्रित था। )

questions.question: Which technological advancement is credited with enabling major breakthroughs in modern AI ?
questions.solution: Machine learning, especially deep learning ( Passage से ली गई Line/s - however, the true inflection point arrived with advancements in machine learning, particularly deep learning, which leverages neural networks inspired by the human brain./ हालाँकि , वास्तविक inflection point मशीन लर्निंग , विशेष रूप से deep learning में प्रगति के साथ आया , जो मानव मस्तिष्क से प्रेरित neural networks का लाभ उठाता है। )

questions.question: According to the passage, what is meant by the 'black box' problem in AI ?
options: The physical design of AI hardware
The difficulty in understanding how complex AI models make decisions
The proprietary nature of AI software
The use of AI in classified government operations
questions.solution: The difficulty in understanding how complex AI models make decisions ( Passage से ली गई Line/s - the 'black box' problem, where the decision-making processes of complex AI models are opaque, further complicates accountability./'black box' समस्या , जहाँ जटिल AI मॉडलों की निर्णय लेने की प्रक्रियाएं अस्पष्ट हैं , जवाबदेही को और जटिल बनाती है। )

questions.question: What ethical concern is not explicitly mentioned in the passage regarding AI's rapid proliferation ?
questions.solution: Environmental impact search on TG -@freepdfhall ( Passage से ली गई Line/s - concerns about job displacement, algorithmic bias, privacy invasion, and the potential for autonomous weapons systems loom large./ नौकरी विस्थापन , एल्गोरिदम संबंधी पूर्वाग्रह , गोपनीयता का हनन , तथा स्वायत्त हथियार प्रणालियों की संभावना के बारे में चिंताएं बहुत बड़ी हैं। )

questions.question: What is presented as crucial for ensuring a benevolent future for AI ?
options: Limiting AI development to government agencies
Allowing market forces to self-regulate AI
A collaborative, interdisciplinary approach to development and policy
Focusing solely on military applications of AI
questions.solution: A collaborative, interdisciplinary approach to development and policy ( Line/s from the passage - a collaborative, interdisciplinary approach involving policymakers, technologists, ethicists, and the public will be crucial in steering AI towards a benevolent future./ एक सहयोगात्मक , अंतःविषय दृष्टिकोण जिसमें नीति निर्माता , प्रौद्योगिकीविद् , नीतिशास्त्रज्ञ और जनता शामिल हैं , AI को एक उदार भविष्य की ओर ले जाने में महत्वपूर्ण होगा। )

questions.question: What is the central issue discussed in the passage ?
options: Expansion of the smart city mission
Lack of employment in rural areas
questions.solution: Unsustainable and unequal urban growth दिया गया passage इस बात पर प्रकाश डालता है कि भारत का शहरीकरण (urbanisation) असंतुलित और पर्यावरणीय रूप से हानिकारक है , और स्थिरता (sustainability) पर दृश्यता (visibility) को प्राथमिकता देता है। अतः , चर्चा का मुद्दा unsustainable और unequal, urban growth है।

questions.question: According to the passage, what makes the idea of “smart cities” superficial ?
options: Ignoring core issues like waste and housing
questions.solution: Ignoring core issues like waste and housing दिए गये passage में कहा गया है कि smart city का concept सतही (superficial) है क्योंकि यह कचरा प्रबंधन (waste management), पानी की कमी (water scarcity) और आवास (housing) जैसी मूलभूत समस्याओं को अनदेखा करता है।

questions.question: What consequence arises from unequal resource distribution across cities ?
options: Overcrowded big cities and declining small towns
Lower cost of living in metropolitan areas
questions.solution: Overcrowded big cities and declining small towns संसाधनों (resources) का असमान वितरण (unequal distribution) भीड़भाड़ वाले महानगरीय क्षेत्रों (overcrowded metropolitan areas) और उपेक्षित छोटे शहरों (neglected smaller towns) की ओर ले जाता है , जिससे एक क्षेत्रीय असंतुलन (regional imbalance) पैदा होता है।

questions.question: What, according to the author, should be the focus of urban design ?
questions.solution: Human-centred inclusivity लेखक इस बात पर जोर देता है कि शहरों को लोगों और समावेशिता (inclusivity) के इर्द - गिर्द डिजाइन किया जाना चाहिए , न कि वाहनों (vehicles) या विशिष्टता (exclusivity) के इर्द - गिर्द , जो मानव - केंद्रित दृष्टिकोण (human-focused approach) को इंगित करता है।

questions.question: According to the passage, what must replace the current approach to achieve meaningful urban transformation?
options: Shifting focus from appearance to genuine outcomes
questions.solution: Shifting focus from appearance to genuine outcomes दिया गया passage इस बात पर जोर देता है कि वर्तमान शहरी नीतियां (current urban policies) टिकाऊ परिणामों (sustainable result) के बजाय दिखावे पर जोर देती हैं। लेखक प्रगति के सतही प्रदर्शनों (superficial displays) से हटकर ऐसी नीतियों की ओर बढ़ने की वकालत करता है जो वास्तविक , मापने योग्य परिणाम (measurable outcomes) देती हैं। asurable outcomes.

questions.question: What was the main objective of British industrial policy in India according to the passage ?
questions.solution: Supporting British economic interests ( Passage से ली गयी Line/s - The colonial economy thus functioned as an appendage to British industry — structured to benefit the metropolis while impoverishing the colony./ औपनिवेशिक अर्थव्यवस्था इस प्रकार ब्रिटिश उद्योग के एक सहायक के रूप में कार्य करती थी - जिसे महानगर को लाभ पहुँचाने और उपनिवेश को गरीब बनाने के लिए संरचित किया गया था। )

questions.question: What does the passage suggest about the railways under British rule ?
options: They were built primarily for social reform.
They aimed to enhance colonial control and extraction.
questions.solution: They aimed to enhance colonial control and extraction ( Passage से ली गयी Line/s - The railways, often celebrated as symbols of modernization, primarily served the colonial purpose of resource extraction and troop movement rather than national integration./ रेलवे , जिसे अक्सर आधुनिकीकरण के प्रतीकों के रूप में सराहा जाता है , मुख्य रूप से राष्ट्रीय एकीकरण के बजाय संसाधन निष्कर्षण और सैनिको के आवागमन के औपनिवेशिक उद्देश्य की पूर्ति करता था। )

questions.question: The term “ deindustrialization ” in the passage refers to—
options: Growth of British manufacturing in India
questions.solution: The collapse of indigenous industries. दिए गए passage में उल्लेख किया गया है कि स्थानीय उद्योगों (local industries) के पतन ने कारीगरों (artisans) को विस्थापित कर दिया तथा बड़े पैमाने पर बेरोजगारी (unemployment) पैदा की। यह विऔद्योगीकरण (deindustrialization) का वर्णन करता है , जिसका अर्थ है औपनिवेशिक आर्थिक नीतियों (colonial economic policies) और ब्रिटिश निर्मित वस्तुओं (British manufactured goods) के प्रवाह के कारण भारत के पारंपरिक हस्तशिल्प (traditional handicraft) एवं कपड़ा उद्योगों (textile industries) का टूटना (breakdown) या विनाश (destruction) । अतः , option (b) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: Why were Indian entrepreneurs limited in their success during colonial rule ?
options: They faced an unsupportive domestic market
They operated within a biased system favoring British capital
questions.solution: They operated within a biased system favoring British capital (Passage से ली गयी Line/s - Even the emergence of Indian entrepreneurs in the late nineteenth century occurred within a highly restrictive framework that favored British capital./ उन्नीसवीं सदी के अंत में भारतीय उद्यमियों का उदय भी एक अत्यधिक प्रतिबंधात्मक ढांचे के भीतर हुआ , जिसने ब्रिटिश पूंजी का पक्ष लिया। )

questions.question: What long-term impact of colonial economic policy does the passage highlight ?
options: Decline of British influence in global trade
questions.solution: Enduring structural economic inequalities ( Passage से ली गयी Line/s - This economic pattern laid the foundation for structural inequalities that persisted long after independence./ इस आर्थिक पैटर्न ने संरचनात्मक असमानताओं की नींव रखी जो स्वतंत्रता के लंबे समय बाद भी बनी रहीं। )

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the author’s overall perspective on air pollution management?
options: It can be resolved through strict emergency measures.
It requires a comprehensive, data-driven, and collaborative approach.
It depends mainly on international financial assistance.
It should be left to technological innovation alone.
questions.solution: It requires a comprehensive, data-driven, and collaborative approach लेखक इस बात पर जोर देता है कि air pollution को isolate करने या temporary action से solve नहीं किया जा सकता है। इसके लिए एकीकृत नीतियों (integrated policies), वैज्ञानिक निगरानी (scientific monitoring) तथा government और citizen के संयुक्त प्रयासों की आवश्यकता है , जो एक व्यापक एवं सहयोगात्मक दृष्टिकोण को इंगित करता है।

questions.question: What is identified as a key limitation in current air quality governance?
options: Excessive reliance on public awareness campaigns
Lack of accurate and continuous monitoring systems
questions.solution: Lack of accurate and continuous monitoring systems दिया गया passage इस बात पर प्रकाश डालता है कि कई शहरों में विश्वसनीय (reliable), वास्तविक समय वायु गुणवत्ता डेटा (real-time air quality data) की कमी है , जो प्रवर्तन (enforcement) और योजना (planning) में बाधा डालता है। इस प्रकार , अपर्याप्त निगरानी को pollution management में एक प्रमुख कमी के रूप में पहचाना जाता है।

questions.question: How does the author propose to reduce vehicular emissions effectively?
options: By introducing more diesel-run public buses
By encouraging private car ownership search on TG -@freepdfhall
Through electric mobility, efficient public transport, and vehicle phase-outs
By constructing more highways and flyovers
questions.solution: Through electric mobility, efficient public transport, and vehicle phase-outs लेखक प्रभावी ढंग से उत्सर्जन (emissions) को नियंत्रित करने के लिए electric vehicles को बढ़ावा देने , public transport का विस्तार करने और पुराने प्रदूषक वाहनों को हटाने जैसे दीर्घकालिक परिवहन सुधारों (long-term transport reforms) का सुझाव देता है।

questions.question: What does the author suggest about public participation in air quality improvement ?
options: It is secondary to technological interventions.
It can drive meaningful change through behavioural adjustments.
It is only effective during pollution emergencies.
It has little impact compared to industrial policies.
questions.solution: It can drive meaningful change through behavioural adjustments दिया गया passage नोट करता है कि नागरिकों के कार्य — जैसे अपशिष्ट जलाना कम करना और ऊर्जा संरक्षण करना (conserving energy) — सीधे air quality को प्रभावित करते हैं , जिससे वास्तविक प्रगति के लिए सार्वजनिक भागीदारी (public participation) महत्वपूर्ण हो जाती है।

questions.question: Which statement reflects the author’s view of the long-term solution to air pollution ?
options: Clean air can be achieved only by halting industrial growth.
Coordinated regional policies and community engagement are crucial.
The issue can be addressed by focusing solely on local governance.
questions.solution: Coordinated regional policies and community engagement are crucial दिया गया passage बताता है कि बेहतर वायु गुणवत्ता (air quality) प्राप्त करने के लिए integrated, cross-border cooperation की आवश्यकता है , जिसमें shared airsheds, joint inventories और early warning systems जैसी व्यवस्थाएँ शामिल हैं। इसके साथ ही civic participation search on TG -@freepdfhall यानी नागरिकों की भागीदारी पर भी ज़ोर दिया गया है। इसका अर्थ है कि लंबे समय तक वायु गुणवत्ता में सुधार के लिए coordinated regional action और community engagement दोनों का मिलाजुला प्रयास आवश्यक है।

questions.question: What is the central argument of the passage ?
options: Technology has replaced human judgment entirely
Truth in the digital age is shaped by algorithms and personal biases
Social media platforms are neutral spaces for free speech
The internet has reduced global communication barriers
questions.solution: Truth in the digital age is shaped by algorithms and personal biases दिए गए passage का central argument इस बात पर प्रकाश डालता है कि digital platform और व्यक्तिगत पूर्वाग्रह (personal biases) अब यह प्रभावित करते हैं कि सत्य को कैसे माना जाता है , तथा वस्तुनिष्ठ (objective) और सत्यापन योग्य समझ (verifiable understanding) की जगह ले रहे हैं। अतः , option (b) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: According to the passage, what drives the spread of misinformation online ?
options: The emotional and viral nature of online engagement
questions.solution: The emotional and viral nature of online engagement दिए गए passage में कहा गया है कि “Social media platform... विश्वसनीयता (credibility) से नहीं बल्कि सहभागिता जुड़ाव के मानकों से आवाज़ को बुलंद करते हैं। परिणामस्वरूप , भावनात्मक तीव्रता (emotional intensity) अक्सर साक्ष्य पर भारी पड़ जाती है , और वायरल होना वैधता का स्थान ले लेता है। ” यह स्पष्ट रूप से दर्शाता है कि misinformation इसलिए फैलती है क्योंकि emotional और viral content को अधिक सहभागिता मिलती है , न कि regulation या journalist की कमी के कारण। अतः , option (b) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: What does the author suggest about confirmation bias?
options: It encourages exposure to new ideas
It causes individuals to seek information that supports their beliefs
questions.solution: It causes individuals to seek information that supports their beliefs ( Passage से ली गयी Line/s - The modern individual, inundated by competing narratives, gravitates toward information that aligns with pre-existing beliefs—a psychological phenomenon known as confirmation bias./ आधुनिक व्यक्ति , प्रतिस्पर्धी आख्यानों से अभिभूत होकर , ऐसी जानकारी की ओर आकर्षित होता है जो पहले से मौजूद विश्वासों के साथ मेल खाती है - एक मनोवैज्ञानिक घटना जिसे पुष्टि पूर्वाग्रह के रूप में जाना जाता है। )

questions.question: What does the author identify as the essence of true digital literacy ?
options: Staying constantly updated on trending topics
Critically analyzing information and opposing views
questions.solution: Critically analyzing information and opposing views ( Passage से ली गयी Line/s - True digital literacy, therefore, lies not in constant connectivity but in critical awareness —questioning sources, examining intent, and embracing discomfort in confronting opposing viewpoints./ इसलिए , ट्रू डिजिटल लिटरेसी निरंतर संपर्क में नहीं , बल्कि आलोचनात्मक जागरूकता में निहित है - स्रोतों पर सवाल उठाना , इरादे की जांच करना , तथा विरोधी दृष्टिकोणों का सामना करने में असुविधा को स्वीकार करना। )

questions.question: Which tone best reflects the author’s perspective ?
questions.solution: Critical and analytical लेखक गंभीर रूप से जांच करता है कि डिजिटल प्लेटफॉर्म सत्य को कैसे विकृत (distort) करते हैं तथा analytically एल्गोरिदम , पूर्वाग्रहों (biases) और महत्वपूर्ण जागरूकता (critical awareness) की कमी जैसे कारणों को समझाता है जबकि पाठको (readers) से जिम्मेदारी से सोचने का आग्रह करता है। अतः , option (a) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: Which of the following best expresses the central idea of the passage?
options: The passage warns against the economic exploitation of natural resources.
It examines how technological and social changes have commodified time and altered human experience.
It explores how pre-industrial societies failed to measure time accurately.
It argues that leisure and work should be treated as identical pursuits.
questions.solution: It examines how technological and social changes have commodified time and altered human experience. दिया गया passage बताता है कि कैसे technology और social evolution ने time को एक प्राकृतिक लय (natural rhythm) से एक मापने योग्य और मुद्रीकृत वस्तु (monetized commodity) में बदल दिया है , जिससे मानवीय अनुभव (human experience) और धारणा (perception) प्रभावित हुई है।

questions.question: What does the author mean by the statement, “Humanity’s obsession with saving time paradoxically ensures that there is never enough of it”?
options: Saving time allows people to be more productive.
The more people try to optimize time, the more they feel deprived of it.
People have stopped measuring time altogether.
The industrial era made time infinite and abundant.
questions.solution: The more people try to optimize time, the more they feel deprived of it. लेखक का सुझाव है कि समय बचाने के प्रति आधुनिक जुनून (modern obsession) केवल अधिक दबाव (greater pressure) और असंतोष (dissatisfaction) की ओर ले जाता है , क्योंकि दक्षता (efficiency ) की चाहत लोगों को समय का सही मायने में अनुभव करने या आनंद लेने से रोकती है।

questions.question: According to the passage, how did pre-industrial societies experience time differently from modern societies?
options: They ignored time completely and lived without schedules.
They perceived time as cyclical and harmonious with nature rather than mechanical and fragmented.
They used more accurate systems for tracking time.
They prioritized personal ambition over collective harmony.
questions.solution: They perceived time as cyclical and harmonious with nature rather than mechanical and fragmented. आधुनिक डिजिटल युग (modern digital age) में समय की रेखीय (linear), यांत्रिक धारणा (mechanical perception) के विपरीत , पूर्व - औद्योगिक समाज (Pre-industrial society) समय को चक्रीय (cyclical) तथा प्रकृति (nature) एवं समुदाय (community) के साथ अंतर्संबंधित मानते थे।

questions.question: What does the passage imply about the effect of hyper-connectivity on human behavior?
options: It strengthens patience and long-term focus.
It fosters calm reflection and silence.
It creates impatience, anxiety, and a constant sense of urgency.
It restores the contemplative rhythm of life.
questions.solution: It creates impatience, anxiety, and a constant sense of urgency. दिया गया passage बताता है कि instant connectivity और constant communication अधीरता (impatience) और चिंता (anxiety) पैदा करते हैं , चिंतन और शांति के स्थान पर निरंतर तात्कालिकता (perpetual urgency) की भावना पैदा करते हैं।

questions.question: Which of the following best captures the author’s proposed remedy for modern alienation from time?
options: Strictly limiting the use of digital technology.
Increasing productivity through better time management techniques.
Reimagining time as a space for presence, reflection, and meaning rather than as a commodity.
Returning to pre-industrial methods of labor and agriculture.
questions.solution: Reimagining time as a space for presence, reflection, and meaning rather than as a commodity. लेखक (author) का प्रस्ताव है कि व्यक्तियों को समय को एक वस्तु (commodity) के रूप में परिभाषित नहीं करना चाहिए जिसे उपभोग (consume) किया जाए बल्कि उपस्थिति , चिंतन और संतुलन के साथ अनुभव करने के लिए एक सार्थक अनुभव (meaningful experience) के रूप में परिभाषित करना चाहिए।

questions.question: What main distinction does the passage draw between justice and equality ?
options: Justice is about fairness, while equality ensures sameness in treatment.
Equality focuses on merit, while justice ignores it.
Both are identical in democratic societies.
Equality is more important than justice.
questions.solution: Justice is about fairness, while equality ensures sameness in treatment. ( Passage से ली गयी Line/s - Justice concerns itself with fairness — ensuring that individuals receive what they are due, based on merit, need, or equity. Equality, on the other hand, demands that all individuals be treated the same, without discrimination or privilege./ न्याय निष्पक्षता से संबंधित है — यह सुनिश्चित करना कि व्यक्तियों को उनकी योग्यता , आवश्यकता या समानता के आधार पर उनका हक़ मिले। दूसरी ओर , समानता यह माँग करती है कि सभी व्यक्तियों के साथ बिना किसी भेदभाव या , विशेषाधिकार के समान व्यवहार किया जाए। )

questions.question: Why does the author mention Aristotle and Rawls?
options: To show how philosophers disagreed on democracy.
To highlight historical and modern perspectives on justice.
To argue that equality has always been superior to justice.
questions.solution: To highlight historical and modern perspectives on justice. ( न्याय पर ऐतिहासिक और आधुनिक दृष्टिकोणों को उजागर करना। ) लेखक Aristotle और Rawls का संदर्भ देते हुए यह दर्शाते हैं कि कैसे विभिन्न युगों के विचारकों ने न्याय की अवधारणा की जांच की है। Aristotle ने आनुपातिक निष्पक्षता (proportional fairness) पर ध्यान केंद्रित किया , जबकि Rawls ने "veil of ignorance" के माध्यम से निष्पक्षता (fairness) का विचार पेश किया , जो समय के साथ न्याय पर विचार के विकास को दर्शाता है। अतः , option (b) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: According to the passage, what problem arises from bureaucratic implementation of justice?
options: It simplifies complex ideas into rigid procedures.
questions.solution: It simplifies complex ideas into rigid procedures. ( Passage से ली गयी Line/s - Bureaucratic systems often reduce nuanced principles into rigid procedures, where equality is measured by uniform rules rather than outcomes./ नौकरशाही प्रणालियाँ अक्सर सूक्ष्म सिद्धांतों को कठोर प्रक्रियाओं में बदल देती हैं , जहाँ समानता को परिणामों के बजाय एकसमान नियमों से मापा जाता है। )

questions.question: What concern does the author raise about algorithms in modern systems?
options: They always promote equality and justice.
They remove all forms of bias.
They can perpetuate hidden biases under the guise of neutrality.
They are too costly to implement.
questions.solution: They can perpetuate hidden biases under the guise of neutrality. दिए गए passage में कहा गया है कि नियुक्ति (hiring) या कानून प्रवर्तन (law enforcement) जैसे क्षेत्रों में एल्गोरिदम " निष्पक्षता का दावा करते हैं लेकिन अक्सर डेटा में एन्कोडेड मौजूदा पूर्वाग्रहों को पुन : उत्पन्न करते हैं। " इसका अर्थ है कि हालांकि वे neutral प्रतीत होते हैं , वे वास्तव में भेदभाव (discrimination) को जारी रख सकते हैं या खराब कर सकते हैं , प्रक्रियाओं search on TG -@freepdfhall को निष्पक्ष (fair) दिखाते हुए अन्यायपूर्ण (unjust) परिणाम पैदा कर सकते हैं। अतः , option (c) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: What is the passage’s overall message regarding equality and justice?
options: Societies must pursue fairness that adapts to context rather than mechanical equality.
Justice is less important than maintaining equal rules.
Philosophical reflection has no practical value.
questions.solution: Societies must pursue fairness that adapts to context rather than mechanical equality. दिए गए passage में इस बात पर प्रकाश डाला गया है कि समानता (equality) का अर्थ है सभी के साथ समान व्यवहार करना , लेकिन सच्चा न्याय (true justice) ऐसी निष्पक्षता की मांग करता है जो विभिन्न परिस्थितियों और असुविधाओं को ध्यान में रखे। यह सुझाव देता है कि समाजों को समानता का लक्ष्य रखना चाहिए , जो कठोर , समान नियमों का पालन करने के बजाय सहानुभूति (empathy) और अनुकूलनशीलता (adaptability) के माध्यम से निष्पक्षता (fairness) और समानता (equality) को संतुलित करता है। अतः , option (a) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of “restorative” as per the given sentence from the passage: Communities that measure value by output tend to ignore the restorative role of stillness.
questions.solution: Exhausting ( थका देने वाला ) - making one very tired and drained of energy. Restorative ( शक्तिवर्धक ) - having the ability to make one feel strong or healthy again. Energizing ( ऊर्जावान ) - giving energy or vitality. Refreshing ( ताज़ा करने वाला ) - making one feel reinvigorated or lively. Healing ( उपचार ) - making well or restoring health.

questions.question: According to the passage, how does solitude contribute to emotional health?
options: By isolating people from social judgment.
By helping individuals process experiences calmly and clearly.
By promoting dependence on external validation.
questions.solution: By helping individuals process experiences calmly and clearly. एकांत (Solitude) लोगों को अनुभवों को संसाधित करने और भावनात्मक संतुलन बनाए रखने में मदद करता है।

questions.question: What does the passage imply about people who spend time alone intentionally ?
options: They tend to develop stronger empathy and self-awareness.
They lose interest in meaningful relationships.
They struggle to maintain emotional balance.
questions.solution: They tend to develop stronger empathy and self-awareness. दिए गये passage में कहा गया है कि जो लोग अकेले समय बिताते हैं वे अक्सर अधिक सहानुभूति (greater empathy) और आत्म - जागरूकता (self-awareness) दिखाते हैं।

questions.question: The passage suggests that modern culture undervalues solitude because it:
options: Promotes collective well-being over individual thought.
Equates success mainly with productivity and visible activity.
questions.solution: Equates success mainly with productivity and visible activity. दिया गया passage इस बात पर प्रकाश डालता है कि समाज व्यस्तता (busyness) और उत्पादकता (productivity) से मूल्य मापता है , ठहराव (stillness) के मूल्य को अनदेखा करता है।

questions.question: What is the main reason the passage gives for society’s decreasing ability to experience solitude?
options: Overreliance on digital communication and constant stimulation.
A decline in individual creativity and imagination.
Growing interest in social and cultural interaction.
questions.solution: Overreliance on digital communication and constant stimulation. दिए गये passage में कहा गया है कि डिजिटल संपर्क (digital interaction) और निरंतर उत्तेजना (constant stimulation) ने लोगों की अकेले समय बिताने की क्षमता को कम कर दिया है।

questions.question: What core tension does the passage highlight in India’s development model ?
questions.solution: Economic growth versus environmental sustainability ( Passage से ली गई Line/s - India faces the dual challenge of maintaining growth while mitigating environmental degradation./ भारत को विकास को बनाए रखने के साथ - साथ पर्यावरणीय क्षरण को कम करने की दोहरी चुनौती का सामना करना पड़ रहा है। )

questions.question: According to the passage, what is a key reason for environmental degradation despite policy initiatives?
questions.solution: Weak implementation of green policies दिए गए passage में कहा गया है कि सरकारी नीतियां (Government policies) अक्सर हरित विकास (green development) की बयानबाजी (rhetoric) पर प्रकाश डालती हैं , फिर भी कार्यान्वयन (implementation) असंगत रहता है। यह स्पष्ट रूप से इंगित करता है कि पर्यावरणीय नीतियां (environmental policies) होने के बावजूद , खराब या कमजोर कार्यान्वयन पर्यावरणीय विघटन (environmental degradation) जारी रहने का एक प्रमुख कारण है। अतः , option (b) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।

questions.question: What environmental issue primarily affects rural communities, as mentioned in the passage ?

questions.solution: Self-examination दिए गये passage में word “introspection” का अर्थ है अपने विचारों , भावनाओं और उद्देश्यों का आत्म - अवलोकन और मूल्यांकन करना। Self-examination इस अर्थ को सटीक रूप से व्यक्त करता है।

questions.question: What is the central philosophical significance of environmental stewardship as described in the passage ?

questions.solution: It challenges anthropocentric assumptions and fosters ecological empathy. दिए गए passage में कहा गया है कि पर्यावरणीय प्रबंधन (environmental stewardship) " मानव - केंद्रित प्रतिमानों (anthropocentric paradigm) पर प्रश्न उठाता है , इस धारणा को चुनौती देता है कि मानवीय आवश्यकताएं स्वचालित रूप से पारिस्थितिक सीमाओं (ecological thresholds) को पार कर जाती हैं " । यह "ecological empathy" एवं गैर - मानवीय जीवन (nonhuman life) के आंतरिक मूल्य को पहचानने के महत्व पर भी प्रकाश डालता है। अतः , option (c) सबसे उपयुक्त उत्तर है।
